faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our clinic and services.
faq.appointments1Question: How do I book an appointment?
faq.appointments1Answer: You can book online through our website, call us during office hours, or visit in person. Same-day appointments may be available depending on availability.
faq.appointments2Question: Can I cancel or reschedule my appointment?
faq.appointments2Answer: Yes. Please cancel or reschedule at least 24 hours in advance by phone or through your patient portal so we can offer the slot to another patient.
faq.appointments3Question: What should I bring to my first appointment?
faq.appointments3Answer: Bring a valid ID, your insurance card (if applicable), a list of current medications, and any relevant medical records or imaging from other providers.
faq.appointments4Question: How far in advance can I book an appointment?
faq.appointments4Answer: You can typically book up to 3 months in advance. For follow-ups or specialist visits we may offer earlier or later windows depending on availability.
faq.appointments5Question: Do you offer reminder calls or messages before my appointment?
faq.appointments5Answer: Yes. We send appointment reminders by phone, email, or SMS (based on your preference) usually 24–48 hours before your visit to help you stay on schedule.
faq.appointments6Question: What happens if I miss my appointment?
faq.appointments6Answer: If you miss an appointment without canceling, we may apply a no-show policy. Please contact us as soon as you know you cannot make it so we can reschedule and free the slot for others.
faq.services1Question: What services does the clinic offer?
faq.services1Answer: We offer a wide range of general and specialist care including check-ups, preventive care, diagnostics, and treatment plans. Specific services are listed on our Services page.
faq.services2Question: Do I need a referral to see a specialist?
faq.services2Answer: It depends on your insurance. Some plans require a referral from your primary care provider. Our front desk can help you check your coverage and arrange referrals if needed.
faq.services3Question: Are lab and imaging available on-site?
faq.services3Answer: Yes. We have on-site lab and imaging facilities for common tests and X-rays. For specialized imaging we may refer you to a trusted partner and coordinate your results.
faq.services4Question: Do you offer telehealth or video consultations?
faq.services4Answer: Yes. We offer telehealth for suitable follow-ups and minor concerns. When you book, choose a video visit option if available; we will send you a secure link before the appointment.
faq.services5Question: Can I request a specific doctor or provider?
faq.services5Answer: Yes. When booking you can request a preferred provider when availability allows. Our team will do their best to accommodate your choice or suggest an alternative.
faq.services6Question: Are same-day or walk-in appointments available?
faq.services6Answer: Same-day appointments may be available depending on schedule and urgency. We recommend calling in the morning to check. Walk-ins are subject to capacity; booking ahead is always preferred.
faq.insurance1Question: Which insurance plans do you accept?
faq.insurance1Answer: We accept most major health plans. Please contact our billing office or check our website for the current list. We can also verify your benefits before your visit.
faq.insurance2Answer: Yes. For out-of-pocket costs we offer payment plans. Our billing team will work with you to find an option that fits your budget.
faq.insurance3Question: What if I don't have insurance?
faq.insurance3Answer: We welcome uninsured patients. We offer self-pay rates and can discuss payment options and any available discounts during your visit.
faq.insurance4Question: How do I submit a claim or get a receipt for reimbursement?
faq.insurance4Answer: We can provide itemized receipts and coded documentation for you to submit to your insurer. Our billing team can also help with direct submission where your plan allows.
faq.insurance5Question: Will you bill my insurance directly?
faq.insurance5Answer: Yes. For plans we participate with we typically bill directly. You may be responsible for copays, deductibles, or non-covered services as per your plan. We will clarify before your visit when possible.
faq.insurance6Question: What payment methods do you accept?
faq.insurance6Answer: We accept major credit and debit cards, HSA/FSA cards, and sometimes cash or check. Payment is typically collected at the time of service for any patient responsibility.
faq.general1Question: Where is the clinic located and what are the hours?
faq.general1Answer: Our address and opening hours are on the Contact page. We are open weekdays and Saturday mornings; emergency care may have different hours.
faq.general2Answer: Yes. Free parking is available for patients in our lot. Accessible spaces are located near the main entrance.
faq.general3Question: How can I get my medical records?
faq.general3Answer: You can request your records in person, by phone, or via our patient portal. We will need a signed release and may charge a small fee for copies as allowed by law.
faq.general4Question: Is the clinic accessible for patients with mobility needs?
faq.general4Answer: Yes. Our building and restrooms are wheelchair accessible, and we have accessible parking near the entrance. Let us know when booking if you need any specific arrangements.
faq.general5Question: How do I contact the clinic in an emergency?
faq.general5Answer: For life-threatening emergencies call emergency services first. For urgent but non-life-threatening matters we have an on-call number; check our Contact page or after-hours message for details.
faq.general6Question: Can I request an interpreter or language assistance?
faq.general6Answer: Yes. We can arrange interpreter or language assistance for your visit when requested in advance. Let our staff know when booking so we can coordinate the right support.
